qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth2 authentication bypass permits unauthorized access to UDM subscriber data and authentication vector generation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an OAuth2 authentication bypass when OAuth2Required=false, which is a different vulnerability class and root cause from the advisory's null byte injection in URL path parameters. The advisory (CVE-2026-33191) specifically describes null byte injection causing 500 Internal Server Error via Go's net/url parser rejection, whereas this finding describes a configuration-dependent authentication bypass. Different vulnerable code locations (context.go:507-518 vs the advisory's path parameter validation), different attack vectors (missing auth vs malformed URL), and different impacts (complete auth bypass vs DoS).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (CVE-2026-33191, GHSA-p9hg-pq3q-v9gv) describes null byte injection in the free5GC UDM service's Nudm_SubscriberDataManagement API, where URL-encoded null bytes (%00) in the 'supi' path parameter cause Go's net/url package to reject the URL with 'invalid control character in URL', resulting in 500 Internal Server Error and denial of service. The oauth2_authentication_bypass finding describes an OAuth2 authentication bypass when OAuth2Required=false, which is a different vulnerability class (authentication vs input validation), different root cause (missing auth check vs improper null byte handling), different code location (context.go:507-518 vs path parameter validation), and different impact (unauthorized data access/modify vs DoS). No PoC execution was needed since the finding does not identify the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause.
evidence: Advisory describes null byte injection in free5GC UDM supi parameter causing DoS (CVE-2026-33191)
oauth2_authentication_bypass finding describes OAuth2 authentication bypass in free5GC UDM when OAuth2Required=false
Different vulnerability classes: input validation/DoS vs authentication bypass
Different root causes: null byte handling vs missing auth check
Different impacts: DoS vs complete auth bypass
